title: marketing — field classification
description: Marketing carries user identifiers dispatched to third-party ad networks (email hashed / phone hashed / click IDs). Value amounts are confidential (competitor tenant cross-inference risk). Provider credentials are RESTRICTED (financial-critical). Consent + attribution snapshots are internal (compliance evidence). No card data (day-pass modeling's payment_method is a category on facility side; marketing carries no payment_method).

confidential: MarketingEvent.value_amount_cents (competitor tenant cross-inference risk — bucketed on egress + never carried in provider webhook payloads)
MarketingEvent.attribution (contains utm_source + utm_campaign — competitor intel; only egressed to admin + marketing role)
MarketingEvent.consent_snapshot (compliance evidence — internal + auditor access only)
MarketingDelivery.error_details (may contain provider response error messages that leak account structure)
MarketingDelivery.provider_event_id (Meta's event_id, Google Ads order_id — feeds provider-side account exploration)
MarketingDeadLetter.resolution_note (ops-authored — may reference internal ticket IDs, workaround plans)
restricted: MarketingProviderConfig.config (ENCRYPTED AT REST — contains pixel_id, access_token, api_key, oauth_token, hmac_secret. NEVER decrypted in logs, notifications, broadcasts, or wire projections. Only the driver factory reads decrypted values.)
MarketingDelivery.request_payload (encrypted at rest — contains hashed PII + attribution + consent snapshot. Support tool decrypts client-side for debugging.)
MarketingDelivery.response_body (encrypted at rest — may contain provider-side identifiers or error details that map back to the tenant's ad account)
regulated_financial: MarketingEvent.value_amount_cents + value_currency (financial audit — 7-year retention; 10 for Enterprise)

MarketingEvent.event_type IN ('subscription_started', 'subscription_upgraded', 'membership_purchased', 'refund_issued', 'chargeback_filed') (revenue events — feeds Wave 4 Finance module revenue attribution)
MarketingDelivery.request_payload (encrypted; contains the dispatched revenue amount)
MarketingDeadLetter.final_error_details (regulator may need to trace failed conversions during a financial audit)
audit_log: MarketingEvent.attribution (frozen at capture; every read audited for compliance)
MarketingProviderConfig.config (every mutation — retention 7 years; contents encrypted in audit blob)
MarketingDeadLetter.resolution_note (audit for ops decisions on paid-conversion losses)

notes: MarketingEvent.attribution jsonb contains utm_* + click IDs. utm_campaign is COMPETITOR INTEL — a tenant's ad campaign names shouldn't leak to other tenants via cross-tenant queries. Enforced by tenant scoping on every read + support-role redaction.
MarketingEvent.consent_snapshot is compliance evidence — a subject can request their historical consent state; export honours GDPR Art. 15 access.

MarketingProviderConfig.config is RESTRICTED (highest classification) — leaked credentials give an attacker the ability to dispatch fraudulent conversions on the tenant's ad account. Encryption at rest is mandatory; the config field is NEVER included in broadcasts, notifications, or audit blobs (audit blob stores the encrypted ciphertext, not the plaintext).

MarketingDelivery.request_payload is encrypted at rest because it contains hashed PII (email, phone) + attribution + consent snapshot. Even though the PII is hashed, correlation attacks against known hashes are possible; storing the payload plaintext would enable such attacks against the DB.
PCI-DSS scope: NO card / PAN / CVV fields on any marketing row. MarketingEvent.value_amount_cents is the currency amount only. Payment references upstream Finance module tokens (Wave 4).
regulated_financial tagging applies to revenue-carrying events + dispatched payloads. The retention pipeline honours the 7-year rule (10 for Enterprise) on this class.
